title: AI candidate-alignment proposer (evidence + confidence -> review queue)
context: OER are abundant and free but are not indexed against the curriculum standards that govern what teachers must teach, so teachers cannot find the free resource aligned to a given standard. This project builds an open crosswalk of educator-reviewed AlignmentRecords linking OER to standards by stable identifier (CASE GUID / ASN URI), not by reproducing copyrighted standards text. Two hard gates never skip: the dual licence/terms gate (gate-002) and educator review of every record, with a blind second-reviewer precision audit holding precision at >= 95%. The AI proposer accelerates candidate generation but never ships a record: educator review remains the ship signal, guarding against automation bias.
objective: Build the AI candidate-alignment proposer that feeds a review queue with evidence + confidence and never auto-publishes.
acceptanceCriteria: Emits (oer, standard) candidates each with alignment type, aiConfidence and a short evidence rationale into a review queue; never auto-publishes a record.
Output is clearly labelled 'candidate - requires educator review'; the reviewer must record evidence/decision (guards against automation bias).
Measured reviewer throughput (records/hour) improves vs. the manual M0/M1 baseline without precision dropping below 95% on the next audit.
Bounded-access protocol respected (no rehosted OER corpus); Code MIT; pnpm build && pnpm test && pnpm lint green; the commit is DCO signed-off; no credentials or secrets committed.
